Title: Govt raises allocation to RM900mil to build 6,000 new classrooms
Article:
IPOH: The government has approved an increased allocation of RM900 million for the construction of 6,000 new classrooms nationwide, up from RM500 million, to address rising demand for school spaces, particularly for Year One pupils.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the additional funding was necessary following a reassessment by the Education Ministry, which reported an increase in student enrolment and classroom requirements.
He said the decision was made to prevent last-minute shortages of classrooms that could disrupt the teaching and learning process.
"In the 2026 Budget, we initially announced RM400 million for 2,500 additional classrooms involving more than 800 schools.
"However, after reassessment and current projections, the government has agreed to increase the allocation from RM500 million to RM900 million to build 6,000 classrooms," he said.
He said this in his speech at the letter of acceptance (SST) presentation ceremony for Bumiputera small contractors at the Perak Education Complex in Meru today.
Also present were Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, her deputy, Wong Kah Woh, and Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.
Anwar said investment in education must remain a priority as it goes beyond literacy, focusing on the development of human capital capable of mastering emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI), while upholding strong moral values and identity.
He also assured that Bumiputera small contractors who perform well would be given opportunities to undertake larger government projects in the future.
Anwar, who is also finance minister, said contractors in the G1 to G4 categories should be encouraged to grow and not remain at a small scale for too long.
He added that performance would be a key criterion in government evaluation for awarding larger contracts.
"If their performance is good, I will ensure that under the government's evaluation system, they are given bigger contracts so they can expand.
"That is my commitment. We do not want them to remain forever as small contractors," he said.
Anwar said the policy to empower small contractors was not merely about opening business opportunities, but about ensuring Bumiputera firms remain competitive and capable of building capacity.
He said the tender ceiling for small projects, previously set at RM500,000, had been raised to RM1 million to provide wider opportunities.
"Previously, we imposed limits to ensure good governance and prevent leakages, but after discussions with officials and industry players, the government agreed to raise the ceiling," he said.
"However, I must stress that opportunities must go to those who are truly qualified, competent and with integrity, not based on connections," he said.
He also directed government departments, including the Education Ministry and the Public Works Department, to give full cooperation to small contractors to ensure faster project implementation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aiman Athirah: 15 teenagers, 1,866 foreigners among 6,680 issued littering notices
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A total of 6,680 individuals nationwide, including 15 teenagers, have been issued notices of offence for littering in public places since January.
Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said the figure included 1,866 foreigners, among them individuals from Indonesia, Singapore, China and Bangladesh.
"So far, 289 individuals from the total have been convicted in court, with 158 having completed Community Service Orders (PKM)," she said at a press conference after officiating the Sinergi Kasih Madani Komuniti Lestari dan Selamat programme at the People's Housing Programme (PPR) Taman Desa Mutiara here today.
Also present was Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) chief executive officer Khalid Mohamed.
Meanwhile, Aiman Athirah said the teenagers, aged between 16 and 18, most of whom committed the offences in Kuala Lumpur, would undergo the same punishment process, including Community Service Orders.
However, she said the PKM must be implemented in accordance with the Social Welfare Department's approach under the Child Act 2001.
She added that SWCorp had also informed the Education Ministry to raise awareness among school students on the enforcement measures.
Aiman Athirah said SWCorp remained committed to carrying out its duties and would not grant exemptions to any individual found committing offences, including minors.
The enforcement under the Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act 2007 (Act 672) came into effect nationwide on Jan 1.
Previously, Khalid reportedly said the government would not compromise with repeat offenders, with stricter action including a maximum fine of up to RM3,000 under local authority by-laws, in addition to penalties under Act 672 and up to 12 hours of community service.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nicotine delisting helped regulate multibillion-ringgit vape industry, says Dr Zaliha
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former health minister Datuk Dr Zaliha Mustafa has defended her 2023 decision to remove liquid nicotine from the Poisons Act 1952, saying the move paved the way to fill a legal gap and bring the multibillion-ringgit vape industry out of the black market.
She said before March 2023, enforcement under the Poisons Act 1952 was insufficient to curb the proliferation of vape products in the market.
Despite being classified as a controlled poison, nicotine-containing vape products were still openly sold on the black market.
"The exemption was a critical step to bring the industry out of the black market so it could be declared, with its supply chain monitored and taxed.
"A multibillion-ringgit industry cannot be allowed to operate without proper records, registration, or monitoring," she said in a Facebook post today.
Dr Zaliha said the matter was also discussed in detail during cabinet meetings at the time.
She said that to address the regulatory gap, the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Bill was tabled in Parliament in June 2023 and has since come into force as the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852).
"The act is a standalone law regulating smoking products, vape devices and nicotine liquids, and it prohibits sales to minors. These areas were previously governed under different laws, including the Food Act and the Poisons Act.
"Malaysia now has a tobacco and vape control ecosystem that is more comprehensive, safer, and better protects the younger generation compared to the previous reliance on outdated provisions under the Poisons Act."
Dr Zaliha said that with Act 852 now fully in force, the legal gap has been addressed.
"On that basis, senior federal counsel argued that the judicial review has largely become academic and no longer has a practical impact on the current public health framework."
Dr Zaliha said she acknowledged and respected the High Court's decision regarding the exemption of nicotine liquids and gels from the Poisons Act, which was made during her tenure as health minister.
"The fact remains that the government and NGOs share the same ultimate goal, to protect the health of Malaysians."
Yesterday, the High Court ruled that the government's decision to
remove liquid
nicotine used in vape and e-cigarette products from the Poisons List was irrational and made without proper consultation with the Poisons Board.
Judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman said Dr Zaliha's decision to exempt nicotine liquids and gels used in vape and e-cigarette products was primarily driven by economic considerations linked to the imposition of excise duties on vape products announced in the 2023 Budget.
Meanwhile, Dr Zaliha said she had been informed that the Health Ministry would file an appeal against the decision.
As such, she said it was still too early to draw any conclusions.
"The appeal process is important to clarify the legal interpretation of the minister's executive powers in making policy decisions in the national strategic interest," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL detains three over illegal parking fee collection in Pudu
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Three men were detained by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) during an enforcement operation against illegal parking fee collectors.
The three local men were detained during the operation at Jalan Yew and Jalan Pasar Baharu in Pudu on Thursday.
The operation was carried out following public complaints regarding individuals allegedly collecting parking fees without authorisation.
In a Facebook post today, DBKL said all three were fined between RM1,200 and RM2,500, in addition to being sentenced to between two and three months' imprisonment after they were produced in court on Friday for offences under Section 50(3) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
DBKL said it would continue monitoring identified hotspot areas and carry out periodic enforcement operations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: Teacher influencers must uphold ethics online
Article:
IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded educators who are active as social media influencers to uphold proper conduct and use their platforms responsibly in shaping public discourse.
He said teachers who engage online should set a positive example by presenting facts clearly and expressing differing views with courtesy, good manners and strong cultural values.
"They should set an example by explaining facts clearly and using proper manners and good culture when criticising, whether they agree or disagree," he said after presenting letters of acceptance to Bumiputera small contractors at the Perak Education Complex in Meru here today.
He said this when asked about the increasing number of teachers becoming influencers.
Anwar said constructive criticism was welcomed, but educators must ensure their influence contributes positively to improvements in society and the education sector.
"That is fine, but they should set a good example so that influencers among educators become a platform for improvement, including in matters related to morals and conduct," he said.
Also present were Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, her deputy Wong Kah Woh, and Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Immigration detains 152 foreigners in Changkat vice raid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 152 foreigners after busting an alleged prostitution syndicate operating behind a three-star hotel in Changkat.
The department said the operation, conducted at 1.30am yesterday, was carried out with the Customs Department following public complaints and two weeks of intelligence gathering by its enforcement division at the Putrajaya headquarters.
A total of 131 Immigration officers of various ranks, along with four Customs officers, were deployed to inspect the hotel and an entertainment outlet suspected of being involved in immoral activities.
Initial investigations found that the hotel had become a hotspot for foreign patrons, particularly Chinese nationals, due to the presence of a luxury entertainment centre allegedly offering prostitution packages involving foreign women.
The department said the operation targeted premises spanning the first to sixth floors of the building.
A total of 243 individuals were screened, comprising 194 foreigners and 49 Malaysians.
Of the total, 152 foreigners aged between 21 and 32 were detained for suspected immigration offences.
They comprised 129 Chinese citizens, two Taiwanese, five Vietnamese, five Myanmar citizens, eight Bangladeshis, and one each from Indonesia, Turkiye and Vanuatu.
The department said the detainees were being investigated under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963, with all of them placed at the Putrajaya Immigration Depot for further action.
Seventeen summonses to witnesses were also issued during the operation.
The department said the entertainment outlet, believed to have been operating for the past three months, was tightly controlled by local bouncers, with access allegedly limited to VVIP customers.
It said room packages were priced from RM50,000, with exclusive rooms costing as much as RM100,000 and available for bookings exceeding 24 hours.
"During the raid, several foreigners were found in a dazed state and engrossed in the loud music, unaware of the officers' presence," the department said in a Facebook post.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt announces 12 initiatives to ease teachers' workload, improve welfare
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government today announced 12 initiatives aimed at improving teachers' career development, welfare and school facilities.
Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said one of the key measures was the abolition of manual co-curricular record-keeping, allowing teachers to focus more on teaching and students rather than administrative tasks.
She said schools would be given autonomy to manage co-curricular records according to their own suitability and creativity before entering students' data into the Physical, Sports and Co-Curricular Assessment System (PAJSK).
"Our commitment is to ensure that
reducing teachers' workload
remains a priority. We want teachers to focus on educational initiatives, classrooms and students," she said during the national Teachers' Day celebration in Ipoh today.
The 12 initiatives announced are:
-Free Google Certified Educator (GCE) Level 1 and Level 2 certification courses for all teachers to strengthen digital literacy skills, with certification costs of RM40 for GCE L1 and RM100 for GCE L2 covered by the government.
-A Special Federal Training Award (HLP) for teachers serving in hardship schools under ProKhas, including Hospital Schools, Floating Schools and rural schools in P1, P2 and P3 areas.
-Federal Teaching Scholarships for school leaders pursuing master's degree studies.
-A RM1 million allocation through sponsorship by YGTHO to strengthen NODE implementation in rural and island schools under the Hardship Schools Programme.
-A RM169 million allocation to provide devices to every school nationwide.
-An additional RM100 million allocation for the maintenance and upgrading of teachers' rooms, bringing the total allocation to RM200 million.
-An additional RM50 million allocation for the maintenance of teachers' quarters, increasing the total allocation to RM110 million to ensure comfortable living conditions.
-The abolition of the manual co-curricular recording system, with schools given flexibility in managing records before submitting information into the PAJSK system.
-A RM10.5 million allocation to provide additional reading materials for all 10,515 preschool classes nationwide.
-A RM122.4 million allocation for the first phase of strategic programmes for schools and students.
-A RM1,000 assistance payment through YGTHO for every teacher whose child scores 10A in the 2025 SPM examination.
-A 50 per cent moratorium on canteen rental payments for six months for parents and communities supporting schools and the ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt on standby over safety concerns involving Sumud convoy, says Anwar
Article:
IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government is closely monitoring developments involving the Sumud convoy amid concerns over the group's safety as it enters a critical phase of its mission.
He said Putrajaya remained on standby and was maintaining direct communication with the convoy to ensure necessary assistance could be provided if needed.
"We are constantly monitoring it. Pressure has already been applied, but we are dealing with Israel, which is among the most uncivilised in the world," he said.
He told reporters this after presenting letters of acceptance (SST) to Bumiputera small contractors at the Perak Education Complex in Meru today.
Also present were Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, her deputy Wong Kah Woh, and Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.
Anwar said Malaysia was prepared to respond should any untoward incident occur involving participants of the humanitarian mission.
"Nevertheless, as a government, we are doing our best. We are also in direct contact with the Sumud convoy to ask what is needed, not only for participants from Malaysia, but also from Spain, Turkiye and Brazil.
"We are prepared should it happen. We pray that it will not happen, but we remain on standby," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested after viral wheelie stunt on Jalan Raja Laut
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 19-year-old man was arrested after posting a video of him performing a wheelie and running a red light on Jalan Raja Laut on social media.
District police Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said officers detected the viral video yesterday on the suspect's Instagram account, which showed him riding a black Yamaha Y15ZR while performing the dangerous stunt and running a red light.
He said the incident was believed to have taken place on April 25 between 2am and 3am on Jalan Raja Laut.
Following intelligence gathering, a team from the department's Intelligence and Operations Unit tracked down and arrested the suspect at about 1.30am on May 16 in the vicinity of Jalan Raja Laut.
He was detained to assist investigations under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act.
A remand application was made, and the court granted one day's remand for further investigations.
Zamzuri said the department took a serious view of dangerous riding and traffic violations that endangered other road users, and reminded the public that roads were not venues for stunts.
"The public is advised to always comply with road laws and not use public roads for dangerous acts in the interest of everyone's safety," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Psychiatrist, police officer recalled in Zara Qairina inquest next week
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A consultant forensic psychiatrist and a police officer will be recalled to continue their testimonies at the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir next week.
Lawyer Shahlan Jufri said consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung of Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang is expected to testify again.
Shahlan, whose legal team represents Zara Qairina's mother, Noraidah Lamat, said they would examine the 67th witness on Monday, as they had previously reserved their questions when the expert testified last month.
Dr Chua had testified before coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan that Zara Qairina's death was most consistent with suicide, and that peer confrontation likely triggered the then 13-year-old's distress before her death.
On July 16 last year, the student was found unconscious on the ground floor of SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha. She was pronounced dead the following day at Queen Elizabeth Hospital here.
Shahlan also said the 70th witness, Assistant Superintendent Mohd Zaidi Abu Hassan, who conducted forensic analysis on 15 mobile phones linked to the case, will continue his testimony.
Zaidi had tendered the contents of his analysis, which included WhatsApp chats, videos and audio recordings.
Meanwhile, Shahlan said his team has prepared three witnesses for the week-long inquest.
"Among them is a child witness who is a family member of Zara's mother," he added.
On the last day of the inquest last month, Shahlan's team told the coroner's court that about 10 to 11 witnesses were expected to be called.
Among the witnesses mentioned were document examiner Dr Linthini Gannetion, handwriting and document examiners Dr Tay Eue Kam and Dr Dzulkiflee Ismail, and forensic scientist Dr Wan Nur Syuhaila Mat Desa.
Additional experts expected to testify include child psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli and psychiatrist Dr Wong Haw Huo.
Zara Qairina's inquest began on Sept 3 following her exhumation and post-mortem examination.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Rumah Bangsa' a platform to defend Malay 'red lines', says KJ
Article:
KOTA BARU: Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative serves as a common platform for various parties to defend "red lines" involving the Malays and Islam, says former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin.
"These red lines cover sensitivities related to Islam, the royal institution, Malay special privileges and the Malay language," Khairy said at an event celebrating Umno's 80th anniversary here.
"Political disputes and rivalries must be set aside when it comes to our core positions on religion and race. If these red lines are challenged, all Malay parties can unite and defend them.
"When we share this stance, no one will dare challenge them."
Khairy said the Rumah Bangsa initiative did not suggest problems with other races, and that relations between the races remained harmonious.
"For example, if an issue involving the Chinese community arises, do MCA and DAP clash? What we see is that they are aligned and united on certain matters involving their community," he said.
It was reported last month that more than 6,000 former Umno leaders and members were readmitted into the party under the Rumah Bangsa initiative.
This included Khairy, the former health minister and Rembau member of parliament.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two motorcyclists arrested over dangerous wheelie stunts on SPE
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two men were arrested after a video shoswing them performing dangerous "wheelie" stunts on high-powered motorcycles along the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) went viral on social media.
In the 30-second
clip
uploaded on Threads, one rider was seen lifting the front wheel of his motorcycle while weaving through traffic as part of a moving convoy on the expressway.
Another rider was also captured performing a prolonged wheelie at high speed, with both men appearing to disregard the dangers posed to other motorists.
The reckless manoeuvres, believed to have been carried out along the SPE, sparked outrage among social media users over the risks to public safety.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief, Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa, said the incident occurred on May 9 at 11am along the SPE from the city centre heading towards Putrajaya.
He said investigations found the two suspects were riding a red Ducati Hypermotard 950 and a black Ducati Panigale.
"The video was uploaded by a Threads user under the account name 'muhdtaufik53'.
"Acting on intelligence and information gathered, a team from the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department Intelligence and Operations Unit arrested two local men aged 31 and 36 at 6am today to assist in investigations," he said in a statement today.
Mohd Zamzuri said police also seized one of the motorcycles believed to have been involved in the incident.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous riding.
"Among the actions taken by police were obtaining CCTV footage from the scene, recording statements from the suspects, documenting the motorcycles involved and applying for remand orders," he said.
Mohd Zamzuri said both suspects were brought before the Kuala Lumpur magistrate's court today, where police obtained a one-day remand order to facilitate further investigations.
He added that the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department viewed such dangerous behaviour seriously and would not compromise with individuals who endangered public safety by violating traffic laws.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor BN set to contest all 56 seats in state polls [WATCH]
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest all 56 state seats in the coming election.
Its chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said this was decided following discussions with the coalition's top leadership and grassroots leaders.
He described the move as a clear commitment to preserve political stability and ensure continued development in Johor.
He said the decision was reached after discussions with the party's top leadership, taking into account the views, aspirations and expectations of Johoreans, as well as Umno and BN grassroots leaders on the coalition's direction ahead of the polls.
"Barisan Nasional Johor will contest all 56 state seats in the coming state election," said the menteri besar.
He said the stand taken by Umno and BN Johor reflected the coalition's readiness to continue forming a stable, strong and service-oriented state government focused on the development of Johor and the well-being of Bangsa Johor.
The coalition remained committed to strengthening public confidence through political stability, effective administration and continued economic growth for the state, he said.
The next Johor state election must be held by April next year, unless the state assembly is dissolved earlier.
The current BN-led administration secured a two-thirds majority in the 2022 Johor state election after winning 40 of the 56 seats.
Onn Hafiz's latest statement is seen as an early indication that BN Johor is moving to consolidate its position amid growing speculation over the timing of the next state polls.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiwan declares itself independent after Trump's warning
Article:
TAIPEI: Taiwan said Saturday it is an "independent" nation, hours after US President Donald Trump warned the democratic island against declaring formal independence.
Trump wrapped up a state visit to Beijing on Friday where Chinese President Xi Jinping had pressed him not to support Taiwan, which China claims is part of its territory.
Taiwan depends heavily on US security backing to deter China from carrying out its threat to annex the island by force.
Taiwan "is a sovereign and independent democratic nation, and is not subordinate to the People's Republic of China", Taiwan's foreign ministry said in a statement.
The ministry also insisted that US arms sales were part of Washington's security commitment to Taiwan, after Trump flagged that he was considering the issue.
Taiwan's statements came after Trump issued a warning to the island against making a declaration of independence.
"I'm not looking to have somebody go independent. And, you know, we're supposed to travel 9,500 miles to fight a war. I'm not looking for that," he told Fox News' "Special Report with Bret Baier."
"I want them to cool down. I want China to cool down," Trump said.
"We're not looking to have wars, and if you kept it the way it is, I think China's going to be OK with that."
The United States recognises only Beijing and does not support formal independence by Taiwan, but historically has stopped short of explicitly saying it opposes independence.
Under US law, the United States is required to provide weapons to Taiwan for its defence, but it has been ambiguous on whether US forces would come to the island's aid.
Xi had begun the summit with a warning on Taiwan, whose President Lai Ching-te considers the island already independent, making a declaration unnecessary.
The Chinese leader told Trump that missteps on the sensitive issue could cause "conflict."
Taiwan's Presidential Office noted Saturday the "multiple reaffirmations from the US side, including President Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio, that the consistent US policy and position toward Taiwan remain unchanged."
"Taiwan-US cooperation has always been demonstrated through action," spokeswoman Karen Kuo said in a statement.
"Taiwan looks forward to continuing to work with the US under the firm commitments of the Taiwan Relations Act."
- US WEAPON SALES -
Ahead of the summit, Trump had said he would speak to Xi about US arms sales to Taiwan, a departure from Washington's previous insistence that it would not consult Beijing on the matter.
Taiwan's parliament recently approved a US$25 billion defence spending bill that will be used for US weapons.
Lawmakers have said the funds will cover nearly US$9 billion of the US$11.1 billion arms package announced by Washington in December and a second phase of arms sales – not yet approved by the United States – worth more than US$15 billion.
Speaking to reporters on Friday en route to Washington, Trump said on arms sales: "I'll make a determination over the next fairly short period of time."
Taiwan's foreign ministry said arms were "not only a US security commitment to Taiwan clearly stipulated in the Taiwan Relations Act, but also a form of joint deterrence against regional threats."
China has sworn to take the island and has not ruled out using force, ramping up military pressure in recent years.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Abdul Rashid dedicates national award to classroom teachers nationwide
Article:
IPOH: Retired educator Abdul Rashid Bakar has dedicated his Teacher's Day recognition to classroom teachers nationwide, saying the honour reflects the quiet but enduring contributions of those who have spent their careers teaching behind classroom doors.
The former Chemistry teacher, who last served at Melaka High School, said he was moved by the award, which he described as both unexpected and meaningful after more than three decades in education.
"I was truly surprised. Throughout my career, we never really expected such recognition. I see this award as representing teachers who are still teaching in classrooms. I am very grateful," Rashid said.
He said this when met after receiving the National Teacher Figure Award from Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in conjunction with the national-level Teachers' Day celebration at a hotel here.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and the prime minister's wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Abdul Rashid received a gold locket, plaque, flight and hotel accommodation vouchers worth RM2,000 sponsored by AirAsia Move and RM50,000 in Premium Savings Certificates sponsored by BSN.
Abdul Rashid, 64, retired on June 1, 2020, after 34 years of service, beginning his career in the early 1980s at a secondary school in Miri, Sarawak, before continuing his service in Melaka, including at SMK Seri Tanjung and later Melaka High School.
He recalled his early teaching years as both challenging and formative, particularly when handling chemistry lessons in schools with limited laboratory facilities.
"Back then, resources were very limited. Chemistry needs proper laboratories, and it was not easy for a young teacher. But those challenges shaped me," he said.
Born in Alor Star, Kedah, Rashid said the evolution of the teaching profession, particularly the introduction of the Excellent Teacher pathway in the 1990s, allowed educators to remain in classrooms while still advancing professionally.
"That was important for teachers like me who love teaching. We did not need to leave the classroom to progress," he said.
The father of three, who was also a recipient of Melaka's Tokoh Guru award in 2020, said he hopes the recognition given to veteran educators will inspire current teachers to remain committed despite growing challenges.
He also praised recent efforts to reduce teachers' administrative workload, saying it allows educators to focus on their primary responsibility of shaping students.
"Teaching is about more than lessons. It is about reaching students' hearts. When you reach their hearts, you open their minds.
"A teacher should be someone who is liked and who makes things easier for others. That is my principle," he said.
Abdul Rashid said the award is not only a personal honour but also recognition of the thousands of teachers who continue to serve quietly, shaping future generations through dedication in the classroom.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palestinian health ministry reports deadly Israeli raid in Jenin camp
Article:
RAMALLAH: Palestinian health officials said Israeli forces killed a man on Saturday on the outskirts of the Jenin refugee camp in the northern occupied West Bank.
The health ministry in Ramallah identified the victim as 34-year-old Nour al-Din Kamal Hassan Fayyad, saying he was "killed by occupation forces' fire in the Jenin camp."
The Palestinian Red Crescent said its teams in Jenin received a man "with no signs of breathing or pulse from inside Jenin camp after he sustained a live bullet wound to the thigh."
The Israeli army did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Israel launched a major military operation in mid-January in multiple northern Palestinian refugee camps, where the army says it is seeking to root out armed groups.
The operation, dubbed "Iron Wall", has targeted Jenin and Tulkarem camps and displaced nearly 40,000 Palestinians, according to the UN agency for Palestinian refugees UNRWA.
The Israeli military has sealed off Jenin camp, allowing displaced residents only limited access to check on their homes and belongings.
Refugee camps were created in the West Bank, Gaza and neighbouring Arab countries after the first Arab-Israeli war for Palestinians who fled or were expelled from what is now Israel at the time of its creation in 1948.
Since the war in Gaza broke out in October 2023, near-daily violence has also rocked the West Bank, which Israel has occupied since 1967.
Israeli soldiers or settlers have killed at least 1,072 Palestinians since then, including many fighters, according to an AFP tally based on Palestinian health ministry data.
Official Israeli figures show at least 46 Israelis have been killed in Palestinian attacks or during Israeli military operations in the same period.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Putin to visit China May 19-20, days after Trump trip
Article:
MOSCOW: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to China on May 19 for a two-day visit, hot on the heels of US President Donald Trump's trip to Beijing, the Kremlin said on Saturday.
During the trip, the Russian leader will discuss with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping how to "further strengthen the comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation" between Moscow and Beijing, according to a Kremlin statement.
Putin and Xi will "exchange views on key international and regional issues" and sign a joint declaration at the conclusion of their talks, it added.
As part of the visit, Putin is also scheduled to discuss economic and trade cooperation with Chinese Premier Li Qiang.
The announcement of Putin's trip comes just after Trump wrapped up on Friday the first visit to China by a US president in nearly a decade, with the grand reception belying a roster of unresolved trade and geopolitical tensions, including over the Ukraine-Russia conflict.
Although Trump and Xi discussed the more than four-year-long conflict – as well as the US leader's stalemated war with Iran – the Republican president took off from China on Friday without appearing to secure a breakthrough on either front.
Negotiations to end the fighting in Ukraine, brokered by the United States, have appeared stalled since the beginning of the US-Israeli war with Iran which broke out on Feb 28.
Before Trump arrived in China, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy asked the US leader to raise the matter of how to stop the conflict during the talks with Xi.
Moscow has ruled out a ceasefire or comprehensive negotiations with Ukraine unless Kyiv caves to the Kremlin's maximalist demands.
- 'DELIGHTED' -
While China has regularly called for talks to end the fighting, it has never condemned Russia for sending troops into Ukraine in February 2022 and presents itself as a neutral party.
Beijing also denies providing Moscow with weapons and military components for its defence industry, blaming Western countries for prolonging Europe's deadliest conflict since World War II by arming Ukraine instead.
As the world's top buyer of Russian fossil fuels, China has become Moscow's key economic partner, especially since Western countries imposed economic sanctions on Russian oil and gas over the conflict.
Trump left China on Friday claiming he had negotiated "fantastic" trade deals, including a commitment for Beijing to purchase 200 Boeing aeroplanes as well as US oil and soybeans.
But the details were vague or scanty, while China made no formal announcement on any trade deal.
In contrast to Trump – who described Xi as a "great leader" and "friend" – Beijing's response to the summit as a whole was more muted.
Russia's top diplomat on Friday welcomed the China-US summit, but said that Moscow enjoyed warmer ties with Beijing.
"If the agreements reached or to be reached by Beijing and Washington are in the interests of our Chinese friends, we can only be delighted," Sergey Lavrov told a press conference in New Delhi.
But Lavrov insisted that Russia was "bound to China by ties... that are deeper and stronger than traditional political and military alliances."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump sees arms sales to Taiwan as negotiating chip with China
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that his pending decision on further arms sales to the island of Taiwan claimed by China was an effective means of pressuring Beijing, reported German news agency dpa.
He said he was leaving the arms deal, worth US$14 billion and already approved by the US Congress, up in the air.
"It depends on China," Trump told broadcaster Fox News.
"It's a very good negotiating chip for us frankly. It's a lot of weapons."
Trump had previously said during his return flight from Beijing to the United States that he would make a decision in the near future.
He said, however, that he first had to speak to Taiwan about it. The US Congress pre-approved the latest arms deal with Taiwan in January 2025.
China rejects such arms sales because it claims Taiwan for itself even though the island republic has an independent and democratic government.
Taiwan was never part of the communist People's Republic founded in 1949.
Nevertheless, China wants to absorb Taiwan. Beijing has already threatened to use military force if this does not work by peaceful means.
The US has long supported Taiwan's efforts to modernise its armed forces and strengthen its defence capabilities.
–Bernama-dpa
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khairy urges BN to contest Johor polls solo to test strength
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Johor state election is the best platform to test BN's strength by contesting solo, former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said.
Khairy, who is also a former health minister, said such a move would allow BN to properly assess its true political strength.
"The next general election (GE) is only a little more than a year away. We will soon hear the drumbeat for state elections in the South, and the dissolution of the Johor state assembly depends on the Regent of Johor.
"In my view, we should test BN's real strength by contesting solo in the Johor state election. We can then see how strong the party really is, while GE is a different matter and can be discussed later.
"I say this because Johor is not a unity government but a BN government, and what we want to do is defend the current administration.
"If the Johor state election results are good, we can extend it to Melaka and other states," he said when speaking at the Umno 80th anniversary thanksgiving ceremony organised by Umno Ketereh here today.
Also present was Kelantan Umno liaison chief Datuk Seri Ahmad Jazlan Yaakub.
Khairy added that Umno must maintain good relations with all parties even when contesting elections.
"Make friends, not enemies. Umno's strength is that we are able to talk to everyone," he said.
Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi had previously said discussions on the Johor state election would only take place after receiving consent from His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail.
He also said whether the Johor state election would be held earlier than the Melaka state election would be discussed with Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia tightens seabass imports, restricts five shrimp species from Thailand
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Agriculture and Food Security Ministry will tighten import controls on fisheries products from Thailand by enforcing the requirement for a Certificate of Analysis (CoA) for siakap (seabass), as well as imposing a temporary restriction on five shrimp species effective June 1.
The ministry said the temporary restriction involves the shrimp species Penaeus esculentes (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
"The restriction on shrimp will remain in place until the Thai authorities submit a complete official response to the questions issued by Malaysia, and is subject to further evaluation by the Malaysian authorities based on the response," the ministry said in a statement today.
It added that the measure is aimed at strengthening national food safety controls, particularly to ensure that seabass and shrimp entering the local market comply with established safety and quality standards.
The ministry said the import control measures will be implemented in an integrated manner through cooperation with the Food Safety and Quality Programme under the Health Ministry, the Malaysian Quarantine and Inspection Services Department (MAQIS), and the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) at all entry points nationwide.
The ministry added that close collaboration with all relevant agencies will continue to ensure the smooth implementation of the import controls without affecting the stability of the seabass and shrimp supply in the country.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman loses over RM50,000 in part-time job scam
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: A woman lost RM55,334 after falling victim to an online scam involving a non-existent part-time job offer.
District police chief assistant commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said on May 11, the 33-year-old civil servant contacted the suspect after coming across an advertisement promoting side-income opportunities on Instagram.
He said the female suspect, who claimed to be an administrative mentor at a company, introduced several part-time job packages offering attractive commission rates.
"The task only required the victim to verify products on a digital platform via links provided by the suspect. The victim completed three initial tasks and received RM1,176 in payments.
"The suspect then persuaded the victim to upgrade her membership package in order to receive higher commissions. However, the tasks had to be carried out in a group via Telegram app, monitored by other suspects," he said in a statement today.
He said the victim made a payment of RM3,350 to access further tasks but failed to receive the promised commission, allegedly due to an incorrect product verification.
"The suspect then instructed the victim to pay RM16,450 to rectify the mistake so that all group members could receive the promised RM32,000 commission.
"One of the group members claimed to be a housewife with a critically ill child. Worried that her mistake would affect other members, the victim made the payment as instructed," he said.
He added that the suspect later informed the victim that the group's commission had increased to RM440,000, however, to withdraw the money, the victim needed to pay an eight per cent tax to the Inland Revenue Board.
"The victim subsequently made payments totalling RM35,534 into two different accounts on May 12 and 15 using her savings.
"She realised she had been scammed after failing to withdraw the money from the digital account provided by the suspect.
"She lodged a police report at 8.12pm yesterday, and the case is being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KJ calls for clear explanation on UEC admission to public universities
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Higher Education Ministry has been urged to provide a clear and continuous explanation regarding the government's decision to allow Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) graduates to enrol in public universities.
Former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin said consistent communication is necessary to prevent public controversy and misunderstandings.
"As announced, I do not see any issue because UEC holders are required to sit for the full Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination. This makes them eligible, though only for certain progammes, and candidates are also required to pass History and Bahasa Melayu.
"However, the ministry needs to explain this matter consistently and in detail, as previous announcements may not have fully reached the public," he said at a press conference today after an event held in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary, organised by Ketereh Umno.
Present was Kelantan Umno liaison chief Datuk Seri Ahmad Jazlan Yaakub.
On Thursday, the Higher Education Ministry, in a statement, said the cabinet had approved
new admission pathways to public universities
for students from outside the national education system, including those from tahfiz institutions, private schools and Chinese independent high schools.
It said the decision reflects its commitment to ensuring inclusive and quality access to higher education for all qualified students, in line with the National Education Philosophy.
For tahfiz leavers, the identified programmes include the Diploma and Bachelor of Da'wah, Diploma and Bachelor of Al-Quran and As-Sunnah, Bachelor of Tahfiz Education, and Bachelor of Islamic Studies (Da'wah).
Meanwhile, for SMPC/UEC leavers, the programmes include the Bachelor of Arts (Chinese Language Studies) with Honours, Bachelor of Chinese Language and Linguistics, Bachelor of Arts (Chinese Studies), and Bachelor of Chinese Language with Education.
The ministry said all applications will be processed based on the principle of meritocracy and academic eligibility requirements, subject to the regulations and decisions of the respective university senates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No info on Jho Low entering Malaysia, says Immigration DG
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Immigration Department has no information regarding allegations that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, entered Malaysia with a high-level Chinese delegation last year.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the department had not received any information related to the claim.
"So far, the Immigration Department has no information regarding the matter," he said when contacted today.
When asked whether the department had any records of Jho Low entering Malaysia, Zakaria declined to comment further.
The allegation surfaced following a report by Sarawak Report claiming that secret meetings involving Jho Low had allegedly taken place in Kuala Lumpur under the protection of a Chinese delegation as part of negotiations linked to 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB)-related debts.
Earlier today, it was reported that police are investigating claims that Jho Low returned to Malaysia for a "
secret meeting
" linked to 1MDB.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri
Mohd Khalid Ismail
said police had yet to verify the claims.
"We will investigate whether the meeting actually took place," he said after attending the police's Autism Day celebration at the Police Training Centre.
On May 13, Khalid said police investigations into the financier were continuing despite reports that he had sought a pardon from United States President Donald Trump.
The Wall Street Journal recently reported that
Low had allegedly sought assistance linked to Trump
in an attempt to obtain relief from legal action in the United States.
He remains wanted by authorities in several countries over his alleged role in the 1MDB scandal.
The WSJ report quoted a White House official as saying Low's request was not currently on its radar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Samsuri replaces Hamzah as new Opposition Leader
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman and Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Moktar has been appointed as the new opposition leader.
Samsuri replaces Larut member of parliament Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, who was
sacked from Bersatu
in February this year.
PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the decision to appoint Samsuri was made during a PN Supreme Council meeting here today.
"The meeting agreed to Samsuri, who is also Kemaman MP, to assume the responsibility as opposition leader," Takiyuddin said in a statement, which was shared on Samsuri's Facebook.
Chaired by Samsuri, the meeting was attended by the PN supreme council members.
Takiyuddin on
April 12
confirmed that Hamzah had submitted his resignation as the opposition leader, saying that it aimed to foster a more conducive political environment and ease matters within PN.
A month before that, Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz urged Hamzah to
step down
as opposition leader after being sacked from the party.
On other matters, Takiyuddin said the PN Supreme Council Meeting had announced the state PN chairman for all states except for Negri Sembilan.
However, no reason was given why the appointment of the Negri Sembilan PN chairman was not made during the meeting.
Takiyuddin also said the meeting discussed electoral cooperation and current issues.
"The meeting agreed in principle to accept electoral cooperation or an electoral pact with selected parties that share similar struggles and common interests with PN.
"The meeting also discussed several current issues of public concern and national interest," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Picnic turns tragic as man drowns, brother goes missing
Article:
KOTA BELUD: A family picnic turned tragic when a 23-year-old man drowned while his younger brother remains missing this morning.
Zul Hakim Abdullah drowned after he attempted to rescue his 18-year-old brother, Daniel, who was believed to have been swept away by strong currents while taking a dip in a river near a campsite at Polumpung Melangkap Baru, here.
His rescue attempt proved futile as he was also carried away by the strong currents.
Kota Belud police chief Superintendent Shahrudin Mat Husain said Zul Hakim was found dead by villagers from Kampung Melangkap before the matter was reported to the Fire and Rescue Department.
"The search and rescue operation for the second victim is still ongoing," he said.
Meanwhile, Kota Belud Fire and Rescue Station chief Elgie Bakuk said the department received an emergency call via the MERS999 line at 9.26am.
Bakuk said a team of nine firefighters, along with an engine from the Kota Belud Fire and Rescue Station, was deployed to the scene, about 30km from the station.
He said the first victim was found by firefighters at 10.18am, about 300 metres from the incident location.
"However, the victim was confirmed dead by Health Ministry personnel before the body was handed over to police for further action and family identification procedures," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Federal Territories PKR Youth chief quits party
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Territories PKR Youth chief has quit the party, citing the party's drift from the reform principles he had believed in since his days as an activist.
"After more than half a term of governing the country, I see this party moving further away from the foundations of the fight I believe in," said Asheeq Ali Sethi Alivi in his announcement on Facebook today.
Asheeq, who is also PKR Batu branch chief, said he had joined PKR shortly after the Pakatan Harapan (PH) government was toppled through the Sheraton Move in 2020, believing at the time that the party needed the energy and idealism of young people and was the best vehicle for change in Malaysian politics.
He added that he had initially believed there was still room to reconcile himself with the direction the party was taking, but found himself increasingly caught between his idealism and what he described as a growing culture of pragmatism within PKR.
"Many decisions made by the government today deviate from the foundations of the struggle I have believed in since my days in activism until now," he said.
He announced his resignation from all party positions effective immediately.
He also said he would serve a two-week notice to the Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh regarding his resignation as Residents Representative Council, and Federal Territories (MPPWP) chairman for the Batu parliamentary constituency.
Asheeq said that his departure from the party would not affect his commitment to the Batu community.
"Whatever my political decisions after this, I will continue my work and service in Batu as a local son born here.
"My commitment to the community has never been tied to any position or party alone," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MEF reaffirms commitment to workplace mental health protection
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) has reaffirmed its commitment to supporting national efforts to strengthen workplace mental health protection and psychosocial risk management.
President Datuk Syed Hussain Syed Husman said the world of work is changing rapidly, and employers are increasingly confronted with psychosocial challenges arising from excessive workloads, burnout, workplace harassment, emotional strain, and blurred work-life boundaries.
"Employers recognise that safeguarding mental wellbeing is essential not only for employees, but also for organizational sustainability and business performance," he said in a statement.
Citing findings by the International Labour Organisation, Syed Hussain noted that psychosocial risks contribute significantly to global economic losses, with cardiovascular diseases and mental disorders linked to workplace stress estimated to account for about 1.37 per cent loss of global gross domestic product annually.
He also highlighted the need for practical implementation support, particularly for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs).
"Employers, especially MSMEs, require adequate time, practical guidance, awareness, and capacity-building support to progressively adopt psychosocial risk management measures effectively.
"The objectives must be to build a culture of prevention, empathy, and shared responsibility across all workplaces," he said.
Meanwhile, social activist Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye, in his keynote address at the MEF OSH (Occupational Safety and Health) Conference 2026 on Thursday, emphasised that psychosocial hazards must be treated with the same level of seriousness as physical workplace risks.
He said a workplace cannot be regarded as truly safe when employees are overwhelmed by unrealistic workloads and exposed to bullying, harassment, or chronic emotional stress.
"These are not merely 'soft issues'; they are genuine occupational hazards that can severely impact wellbeing, safety, and productivity," he added.
Lee also stressed the importance of fostering workplace cultures where employees feel psychologically safe to speak openly about mental health concerns without fear of stigma, discrimination, or retaliation. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar to teachers: Stay positive in face of criticism
Article:
IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on educators to remain resilient in the face of criticism, saying such challenges should be viewed constructively to strengthen the teaching profession and uplift future generations.
He said teachers must continue to play a central role in shaping students' character and values, despite being increasingly affected by social issues that spill into schools.
"Teachers appear to be burdened. There are always issues. Social problems inevitably affect students in schools and naturally cause disruption.
"Many of these challenges reflect broader moral and social weaknesses within society," he said in his speech at the Teachers' Day celebration themed Exemplary Teachers, Heartbeat of the Nation at a hotel here today.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Anwar said that education cannot be separated from the formation of character and human values, noting that the ultimate purpose of schooling goes beyond academic achievement.
"Education is about seeking knowledge, improving understanding, evaluating well, and shaping human beings with values and ethics.
"But this is not happening sufficiently in society, politics, or even social media," he said.
He also acknowledged the pressures faced by teachers, who often become the first point of contact for societal problems affecting students.
"If not teachers, then who else?" he said, calling on educators to continue their role positively even when confronted with unfair criticism.
Anwar added that the education system must balance technological advancement with the preservation of human values, warning against allowing hatred or racial prejudice to take root in society.
He also said it is important to embrace diverse civilisational values, including Confucian and Indian philosophical traditions, alongside Islamic teachings, which he said form the moral foundation of the nation.
"There is no place in education for narrow thinking. In education, we honour all perspectives," he said, adding that mutual respect is key to earning respect from others.
At the event, Anwar presented awards to SK Putrajaya Presint 11(1), which has been named the winner of the World's Best School Prize 2025 for Supporting Healthy Lives; the National Educational Leadership Figure Award to Dr Ahmad Rafee Che Kassim; and the National Teacher Figure Award to Abdul Rasid Bakar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Subsidy reforms not strictly reliant on income classification, says Economy Minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government does not strictly rely on income classifications such as T20 when formulating subsidy policies, saying a broader and more holistic approach is needed in ongoing discussions on subsidy targeting.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry avoids rigid classifications such as "T20", as household income alone does not fully reflect economic reality.
"I have never really used terms like T or this or T that.
"From the Economy Ministry's perspective, it is more relevant to look at things comprehensively because economic comparisons are not just about income, but also essential expenditure and other factors," he told reporters here today.
Akmal said subsidy rationalisation remains part of the government's wider economic restructuring agenda aimed at ensuring assistance is delivered more effectively to the intended groups.
He said the matter is regularly reviewed under both long-term planning, including the 13th Malaysia Plan, and short-term responses to economic pressures.
"From the perspective of the Economy Ministry, what we are trying to achieve, whether through the 13th Malaysia Plan or when facing economic challenges, is the need to restructure the economy into a better state to ensure continuity and resilience."
Akmal said discussions on more targeted subsidy distribution are ongoing, particularly to ensure government programmes reach their intended beneficiaries.
"There are always discussions on making subsidies more targeted because we want programmes and contributions to reach the right groups.
"The challenge is determining the threshold or criteria that can be used."
He said the government has already announced plans to implement subsidy reforms for diesel as part of a broader effort to ensure fiscal sustainability and better targeting.
He said the approach was necessary given ongoing global uncertainties and rising cost pressures.
"We are structuring this in stages so that when we roll it out, we do so in a way that manages rising pressures. We know that as long as global conflicts persist, the burden will continue to grow."
He added that mitigation measures and policy interventions would continue to be introduced from time to time to manage economic challenges.
Critics
have previously argued that the move unfairly paints all T20 earners as financially comfortable, despite many grappling with high living costs, housing commitments, childcare expenses and mounting financial obligations in major cities.
"The best policy is not one that punishes income but one that reduces waste, protects necessity, discourages excess and preserves public trust," said Samirul Ariff Othman, a senior consultant at Global Asia Consulting.
He had said that targeting the T20 risks oversimplifying a highly diverse group while creating the perception that higher earners are being unfairly penalised despite already contributing disproportionately through income taxes.
This follows the Putrajaya plan to consider excluding the T20 income group from the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) fuel subsidy scheme.
Akmal also previously said the government is currently
prioritising
the rollout of the Budi diesel programme.
He said there has been no discussion on any reduction in the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) quota in the National Economic Action Council.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Honouring teachers as the pulse of the nation's future
Article:
LETTERS:
In conjunction with the 2026 Teachers' Day celebration, themed "Guru Bitara Nadi Negara" (Teacher Champions, the Nation's Pulse), we extend our highest appreciation and heartfelt gratitude to all educators across the country for their immeasurable dedication, iron commitment and selfless sacrifices to forge the very soul of Malaysia's future.
This year's theme is more than a mere symbolic expression - it represents a profound recognition of teachers as the driving force behind national development and societal progress.
The role of teachers as catalysts for change and agents of social transformation requires continuous support from all stakeholders.
To sustain and amplify their monumental impact, unwavering and comprehensive support must flow from every corner of the society, from the Ministry of Education (MOE), policymakers to educational institutions and every citizen who cherishes Malaysia's growth and future.
In the era of the Fourth Industrial Revolution and the transition towards a digital economy and artificial intelligence (AI), our national education system faces an urgent need to adapt rapidly to technological advancements and evolving global market demands.
Today's teachers are expected to be more than traditional educators - they must possess technological proficiency, understand the dynamics of digital learning and be capable of fearlessly integrating cutting-edge innovation into every dimension of their teaching.
A key challenge lies in ensuring that the emphasis on future-ready skills does not come at the expense of foundational education, including literacy, numeracy and values-based learning.
There is a real risk that the future-ready agenda may become overly rhetorical if foundational learning disparities, particularly between urban and rural communities, are not addressed systematically.
The Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026–2035, launched by the MOE in January 2026, positions teachers as the primary drivers of national education transformation.
But, long-standing structural issues such as excessive non-academic workloads, assessment pressures and limited opportunities for continuous professional development must be resolved.
Teachers' wellbeing remains a national priority. Findings by the National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP) indicate that mental health concerns among teachers demand sustained, urgent attention.
As such, work-life balance and mental wellbeing must be placed at the forefront of the ministry's agenda as educators who are emotionally, mentally and physically resilient are better equipped to fulfil their responsibilities effectively.
In this regard, MASA urges the MOE to undertake more proactive and systematic measures to strengthen the dignity and standing of the teaching profession through improved access to modern educational infrastructure and technology, continuous competency development opportunities and appropriate recognition for their contributions.
MASA wishes all educators a meaningful and inspiring Teachers' Day celebration. Thank you for your unwavering service, sacrifices and dedication in nurturing the champions and visionaries of tomorrow.
You are not only teachers – you are the unshakeable backbone, the blazing torch and heartbeat of national development.
AZRIL MOHD AMIN
Chief Executive Officer
Institut Masa Depan Malaysia (MASA)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar launches national Teachers’ Day 2026, praises educators’ role in nation‑building
Article:
IPOH, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today officiated the 55th National-Level Teachers’ Day Celebration 2026 held at Hotel Casuarina @ Meru here.
The celebration, themed ‘Guru Bitara Nadi Negara’, reflects appreciation for the role of teachers that goes beyond imparting knowledge, with educators now recognised as builders of the nation’s future.
This year’s main concept aims to uphold teachers’ professionalism in shaping a prosperous society based on the Madani principles of Sustainability, Well-being, Creativity, Respect, Confidence and Compassion.
Also present at the celebration were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, as well as about 3,000 educators from across the country.
In her speech, Fadhlina announced 12 exciting initiatives to empower and strengthen teachers’ duties, including the continuation of free Google Certified Educator (GCE) Level One and Level Two certification courses for all teachers.
In addition, Fadhlina announced the expansion of the Federal Teaching Scholarship to school leaders pursuing master’s degree studies, as well as a special Federal Training Award for teachers willing to serve in hardship schools under ProHas (School Attendance Programme) in P1, P2 and P3 areas.
She also announced an allocation of RM169 million to supply devices to every school next year to further strengthen school co-curricular activities and enable the uninterrupted application of technology in the teaching and learning process.
Also announced was the abolition of the manual co-curricular recording system, with schools to be given autonomy to manage co-curricular recording according to their suitability and creativity before entering students’ co-curricular information into the Physical, Sports and Co-curricular Activities Assessment (PAJSK) system.
Meanwhile, the Prime Minister, who occasionally referred to himself as ‘Cikgu Anwar’ during his officiating speech, stressed the concept of “Excellence Without a Soul”, emphasising that academic intelligence alone is insufficient if separated from humanity, moral values and social compassion.
At the same time, the Prime Minister also shared a personal story about his close relationship with his former teacher and former director-general of education, the late Tan Sri Abdul Rahman Arshad, as a symbol of a student’s deep respect for teachers.
At the same ceremony, Anwar also presented three awards, namely the World’s Best School award to Sekolah Kebangsaan Putrajaya Presint 11 (1), Putrajaya, the National Education Leadership Figure Award to Dr Ahmad Rafee Che Kassim, and the National Teacher Figure Award to Abdul Rasid Bakar.
Perak was selected as the host for this year’s celebration, which was held over two days beginning yesterday, featuring several main events including a royal dinner attended by the Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Shah, as well as the National-Level Teachers’ Awards Ceremony officiated by the Education Minister.
As a mark of appreciation for the contributions and excellence of educators, a total of 32 awards in various categories were presented, including the Education Minister’s Special Award, Tokoh Guru Kebangsaan and Tokoh Kepimpinan Pendidikan Kebangsaan, as well as awards for Innovative Teacher, Distinguished Teacher and Icon categories, such as Digital Content Creator and Distinguished Teacher with Disabilities.
This year’s celebration was not merely an annual event, but also served as an important platform in realising the aspirations of the Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026-2035.
It encompasses improving education quality through the empowerment of 21st-century pedagogy integrating digital technology and artificial intelligence; teachers’ well-being through balancing teachers’ workload, providing psychosocial support and recognising teachers’ expertise; and inclusive education by positioning teachers as agents of equality so that no student is left behind, including students with special needs. — Bernama

Title: Govt to review education ministry allocation, considers increase in upcoming budget, says PM
Article:
IPOH, May 16 — The government will continue to review and consider increasing allocations to the Ministry of Education (MOE) from time to time, including efforts to strengthen digitalisation, artificial intelligence (AI), and Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM).
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said budget allocations will be increased in line with the current needs of the national education sector.
“In my meeting with the Ministry of Finance earlier this week, we also discussed education, as we are now placing emphasis on digitalisation, AI, computing, STEM and so on.
“We have decided to review all requirements so that, in this year’s budget, we can further increase expenditure and approvals for the MOE,” he said when launching the national-level Teachers’ Day celebration here today.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, and Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.
In addition, Anwar said the Madani Government has implemented various initiatives for teachers’ welfare, including maintenance and upgrading of teachers’ rooms in 3,500 schools.
“Teachers’ rooms have been upgraded and maintained to accommodate the growing number of teachers. We have carried out maintenance on 7,000 teachers’ rooms,” he said.
He said following the policy of admitting Year One pupils aged six starting from the 2027 school session, the government had approved the recruitment of an additional 3,150 teachers.
Anwar said more than 4,000 teacher quarters have been maintained, and a total of 8,700 quarters are expected to be maintained through additional government allocations.
Earlier, Fadhlina announced an additional RM100 million for the maintenance and upgrading of teachers’ rooms nationwide, as well as RM50 million for the maintenance of teacher quarters. — Bernama

Title: Govt closely monitoring Malaysia-led Gaza aid flotilla amid fears of interception, says Anwar
Article:
IPOH, May 16 — Malaysia is closely monitoring the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) humanitarian mission to Gaza after the convoy entered high-risk waters where organisers fear possible interception, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today,
He said the government remains on standby as the flotilla, involving 54 ships and nearly 500 activists from 45 countries, approaches areas near Gaza that organisers say carry a high risk of action by Israeli forces.
“We are always monitoring... The government will do what it can.
“We are in direct communication with not only the Malaysian participants but also with Spain, Turkiye and Brazil,” he was quoted by
The Star
as saying after meeting Bumiputera small contractors here today.
“We pray that nothing will happen, but we must be prepared,” he added.
The flotilla, coordinated by the Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC), hs entered what organisers described as a “high-risk zone”.
The latest mission comes after an earlier Global Sumud Flotilla attempt reportedly saw several vessels intercepted in international waters, with activists detained and some boats damaged or allegedly sabotaged.
SNCC previously said 10 Malaysians involved in the earlier mission were among several participants detained several days by Israeli forces last month, and later released in Turkiye.

Title: Onn Hafiz says BN will go solo in Johor polls, rules out electoral pact with Pakatan, Perikatan, and Muda
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 16 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) today confirmed it will contest all 56 seats in the next state election, effectively ruling out any electoral cooperation with Pakatan Harapan (PH), Perikatan Nasional (PN) or Muda.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the decision was made after discussions with the coalition’s component party leadership.
“With a sense of conviction and responsibility, Johor BN will contest in all 56 state constituencies in the upcoming state election.
“For Umno and Johor BN, the decision to contest in all 56 state seats is a clear offer to the people that we are prepared to continue to form a stable, strong government.
“The decision will fully serve the state’s development and the well-being of Johoreans,” he said in a statement today.
Onn Hafiz, who is also the Johor menteri besar and state Umno chief, said the coalition had taken into account the views and expectations of Johoreans as well as feedback from Umno and BN grassroots leaders.
The announcement appears to end speculation over possible seat-sharing arrangements or political cooperation between Johor BN and other coalitions ahead of the state polls.
In the 2022 Johor election, BN won 40 of the 56 state seats, dominated by Umno victories.
PH secured 12 seats, of which 10 were won by DAP and one each by PKR and Amanah.
PN won three seats through Bersatu and PAS, while Muda captured one seat.

Title: Mara to sponsor future pilots, with tahfiz STEM students first in line
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will soon open applications for a pilot cadet financing programme, with priority to be given to tahfiz students from the STEM stream.
Mara chairman Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said advertisements and eligibility requirements for the programme would be announced soon.
“New students who wish to apply for this pilot cadet programme must meet the basic requirements set by flight training academies such as Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) and BATS Aviation.
“Mara-sponsored academies must also have agreements with airlines to guarantee employment opportunities for trainees who receive Mara financing,” he said in a Facebook post today.
Asyraf said the minimum requirement for the programme includes students from the science, technology, engineering and mathematics stream.
“Although the offer is open to all qualified students, priority will be given to tahfiz students from the STEM stream.
“They will go through a selection process, including interviews. The final interview stage will be with the Mara chairman himself for a Quran memorisation assessment through Quran recitation verification,” he said.
He added that the programme is aimed at producing more qualified Bumiputera pilots while expanding opportunities for tahfiz students in the aviation industry.

Title: Perikatan names Kemaman MP Ahmad Samsuri as new Opposition Leader, signals openness to electoral pact
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) has appointed coalition chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as the new Opposition Leader in Parliament.
The Kemaman MP who is also Terengganu menteri besar replaces Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin who was sacked as Bersatu deputy president in February.
“The council agreed to appoint Kemaman MP Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar to shoulder the responsibility as Opposition Leader,” PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said in a Facebook statement this afternoon.
He said the decision was made during the coalition’s supreme council meeting at the Hotel Royale Chulan here earlier today.
“The council agreed in principle to accept electoral cooperation or an electoral pact with certain parties that share compatible struggles and common interests with Perikatan Nasional,” he added.
He also announced PN’s new line-up of state heads ahead of preparations for the next general election, as follows:
Perlis — Datuk Seri Shahidan Kassim
Kedah — Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor
Penang — Datuk Dominic Lau Hoe Chai
Perak — Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu
Selangor — Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali
Federal Territories and Melaka — Datuk Mohd Radzi Md Jidin
Johor — Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal
Pahang — Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man
Terengganu — Datuk Alias Razak
Kelantan — Datuk Ahmad Yakob
Sabah — Datuk Aliakbar Gulasan
Sarawak — Jaziri Alkaf Abdillah Suffian
The only state without a head for now is Negeri Sembilan, which Takiyudddin listed as being “deferred”.
The question over who would lead the Opposition had lingered since Hamzah’s removal from Bersatu earlier this year.
The Dewan Rakyat is scheduled to reconvene from June 22.

Title: Klang hit-and-run leaves two sisters dead and 17-year-old boy injured
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — A family walk to dinner in Bandar Baru Klang, Selangor ended in the death of two women and the hospitalisation of a 17-year-old boy after a hit-and-run along Jalan Pekan Baru at about 7.40pm yesterday.
North Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the deceased were two sisters aged 57 and 62 while the survivor was the son of one of the women,
The Star
reported today.
He said investigations so far indicated that a silver SUV travelling from Jalan Meru towards Bandar Baru Klang crashed into the three pedestrians who were on the road shoulder before fleeing the scene.
He believes the trio were walking from their nearby home to have dinner when the crash happened.
He said both women died at the scene from severe head injuries while the boy had  minor injuries and is receiving treatment at Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital in Klang.
He added that police have yet to record a statement from the teen.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving, punishable with a jail term between two and 10 years, and fined between RM5,000 and RM20,000.
Anyone convicted under this section will also lose their driving licence for at least three years, or 10 years for repeat offenders.
Police are seeking further information and urge those with details to contact the investigating officer Inspector Ahmad Fadzli Abdullah at 017-5843860 or visit the nearest police station.

Title: PM launches Paduri Madani scheme to empower B40 women entrepreneurs with loans up to RM30,000
Article:
IPOH, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today launched the PADURI Madani Scheme under Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM), aimed at empowering women, particularly those in the B40 group, through entrepreneurship.
The initiative, which targets 29,000 new women entrepreneurs, is open to Malaysians aged 18 and above with a monthly household income not exceeding RM5,880 or a per capita income of RM1,470.
